quiz.title: Algebra-2 Unit 1 Quiz 5
quiz.subtitle: Operations with Polynomials
quiz.subject: Algebra 2
quiz.topic: Algebra I Review: 1.5 Operations with Polynomials

questions.question: Simplify: $(5x^3 - 2x^2 + 7x - 4) + (3x^3 + 6x^2 - 5x + 9)$.
questions.explanation: Combine like terms: $5x^3 + 3x^3 = 8x^3$, $-2x^2 + 6x^2 = 4x^2$, $7x - 5x = 2x$, $-4 + 9 = 5$. Result: $8x^3 + 4x^2 + 2x + 5$. Only terms with the same exponent can be combined.

questions.question: Simplify: $(7x^3 - 4x^2 + 2x - 11) - (2x^3 + 5x^2 - 8x + 3)$.
questions.explanation: Distribute the negative: $7x^3 - 4x^2 + 2x - 11 - 2x^3 - 5x^2 + 8x - 3$. Combine: $(7-2)x^3 = 5x^3$, $(-4-5)x^2 = -9x^2$, $(2+8)x = 10x$, $(-11-3) = -14$. The most common error is forgetting to distribute the negative to every term in the second polynomial.

questions.question: Multiply: $4x^2(3x^3 - 2x^2 + 5x - 7)$.
questions.explanation: Distribute $4x^2$ to each term: $4x^2 \cdot 3x^3 = 12x^5$, $4x^2 \cdot (-2x^2) = -8x^4$, $4x^2 \cdot 5x = 20x^3$, $4x^2 \cdot (-7) = -28x^2$. Multiply coefficients and add exponents: $x^m \cdot x^n = x^{m+n}$.

questions.question: Multiply: $(3x - 5)(2x + 7)$.
questions.explanation: FOIL: First $3x \cdot 2x = 6x^2$. Outer $3x \cdot 7 = 21x$. Inner $-5 \cdot 2x = -10x$. Last $-5 \cdot 7 = -35$. Combine: $6x^2 + (21x - 10x) - 35 = 6x^2 + 11x - 35$.

questions.question: Expand: $(2x - 3y)^2$.
questions.explanation: $(a - b)^2 = a^2 - 2ab + b^2$. With $a = 2x$, $b = 3y$: $(2x)^2 - 2(2x)(3y) + (3y)^2 = 4x^2 - 12xy + 9y^2$. The middle term is essential — $(a-b)^2 \neq a^2 + b^2$.

questions.question: Multiply: $(5x + 2)(5x - 2)$.
questions.explanation: $(a + b)(a - b) = a^2 - b^2$. With $a = 5x$, $b = 2$: $(5x)^2 - 2^2 = 25x^2 - 4$. The sum × difference pattern always yields the difference of squares — the middle terms cancel.

questions.question: A student expanded $(x + 6)^2$ and got $x^2 + 36$. What is the correct expansion, and what error did the student make?
options: $x^2 + 12x + 36$; the student forgot the middle term $12x$
$x^2 + 6x + 36$; the student miscalculated the middle term
questions.explanation: $(x + 6)^2 = x^2 + 2(x)(6) + 6^2 = x^2 + 12x + 36$. The student incorrectly assumed $(a + b)^2 = a^2 + b^2$, which is a very common mistake. The middle term $2ab$ is always present when squaring a binomial.

questions.question: A picture frame has outer dimensions $(x + 6)$ by $(x + 4)$ inches. The inner picture is $x$ by $(x - 2)$ inches. What polynomial represents the area of the frame (border only)?
questions.explanation: Outer area: $(x + 6)(x + 4) = x^2 + 10x + 24$. Inner area: $x(x - 2) = x^2 - 2x$. Frame area: $(x^2 + 10x + 24) - (x^2 - 2x) = 12x + 24$. The $x^2$ terms cancel — the frame area grows linearly with $x$.

questions.question: A company sells $x$ units of a product at a price of $(200 - 5x)$ dollars per unit. Write a polynomial for the total revenue $R(x)$.
questions.explanation: Revenue = units × price per unit. $R(x) = x(200 - 5x) = 200x - 5x^2 = -5x^2 + 200x$. The revenue is a quadratic function — as more units are sold, the price drops, so revenue eventually peaks and declines.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered polynomial operations: addition, subtraction, multiplication (monomial × polynomial, binomial × binomial, binomial × trinomial), special products, error analysis, and real-world applications.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review any missed topics: (1) When subtracting, distribute the negative to every term in the second polynomial, (2) $(a + b)^2 = a^2 + 2ab + b^2$ — don't forget the middle term, (3) $(a + b)(a - b) = a^2 - b^2$ — the middle terms cancel, (4) For area applications, subtract the inner area from the outer area.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Key areas to review: (1) Only like terms (same exponent) can be combined, (2) When multiplying, multiply coefficients and add exponents, (3) FOIL for binomials: First, Outer, Inner, Last, (4) Special products save time: square of a binomial and sum × difference are patterns worth memorizing.
scoreMessages.0: This review covers essential polynomial operations. Focus on: adding/subtracting polynomials (combine like terms carefully), multiplying a monomial by a polynomial (distribute to every term), multiplying binomials (FOIL), multiplying a binomial by a trinomial (distribute each term of the first), special products ($(a \pm b)^2$ and $(a+b)(a-b)$), and polynomial applications (area, revenue).
